relationship.description: A USER (sender) can send many EMAILs; EMAIL.sender_id → USER.user_id
An EMAIL may have multiple ATTACHMENTs; ATTACHMENT.email_id → EMAIL.email_id
An EMAIL can have multiple EMAIL_RECIPIENT rows representing TO, CC, BCC; EMAIL_RECIPIENT.email_id → EMAIL.email_id
Each EMAIL_RECIPIENT references a USER who receives the email; EMAIL_RECIPIENT.user_id → USER.user_id

metadata.improvements_made: Added optional email attributes (is_read, priority, thread_id) for status, importance and threading. Added mime_type to ATTACHMENT for file type handling. Renamed EMAIL_RECIPIENT.type to recipient_type for clarity (TO, CC, BCC). Explicitly documented foreign‑key constraints in relationship descriptions. Kept the original clean structure and associative entity to maintain efficiency and query clarity.
